meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to master SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your MVP quickly.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Instead, build a simple SaaS pricing calculator or tier-optimization tool. By focusing on the math of revenue—MRR, churn, and LTV—you solve a tangible pain point for other founders while learning the core architecture of subscription-based software. Start building, then iterate based on real feedback.
content.intro:
The blank screen is a graveyard for ambition. You have the technical stack, the drive, and the vision, yet you remain paralyzed by the search for a unicorn idea. This is the 'Founder's Trap,' where analysis leads to stagnation and the opportunity cost of inaction quietly erodes your potential for market dominance.

The secret isn't a better idea; it is a better process. By pivoting your focus to the infrastructure of monetization—specifically SaaS pricing logic—you transform from a dreamer into a revenue-focused architect. Let’s bypass the fluff and build a functional, high-value tool that solves a real business problem from day one.

steps.title: Define the Value Metric
steps.description: Identify the primary driver of your pricing model (e.g., per-seat, per-API call, or flat-fee). This step forces you to define how your future users will derive value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a boilerplate SaaS pricing model schema in TypeScript that handles tiered subscription logic, including monthly and annual billing cycles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a pricing.ts file defining an interface for TieredPricing and a function to calculate annual savings.'

steps.title: Build the Revenue Calculator
steps.description: Create a front-end component that allows users to input their expected churn and acquisition costs to visualize MRR growth.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component using Tailwind CSS that displays an interactive MRR dashboard based on pricing inputs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npm install lucide-react && claude add component RevenueCalculator.tsx'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe-Ready Logic
steps.description: Structure your code to be compatible with Stripe's Product and Price objects, ensuring your MVP is ready for real payment integration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a utility function that maps our internal pricing tiers to Stripe API payload requirements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the pricing logic to integrate with a mock Stripe service layer.'

steps.title: Implement Pricing Psychology
steps.description: Add logic to highlight the 'Best Value' tier and calculate the psychological discount of annual billing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a feature to our calculator that automatically applies a 20% discount for annual plans and flags the middle tier as 'Recommended'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Add discount calculation logic to pricing.ts and update the UI to show a recommended badge.'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Push your pricing tool to a live URL so you can share it with potential customers for immediate validation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel deployment configuration file and a README.md explaining how to use the pricing tool.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'git add . && git commit -m "Deploy MVP Pricing Tool" && git push'

pro_tips: Always build your pricing logic as a standalone library; you will reuse this exact code in every future SaaS project.
Never hardcode prices; store them in a database or environment variables so you can A/B test pricing without a redeploy.
Focus on the 'Annual vs Monthly' toggle early; it is the single fastest way to improve your cash flow.
Use 'Price Anchoring' in your UI by placing your most expensive tier first; it makes the middle tier seem significantly cheaper.
Treat your pricing page as a conversion funnel; if users don't understand the value in 5 seconds, they will bounce regardless of the price.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, share the tool in relevant founder subreddits and Discord groups. Ask for feedback on the pricing tiers specifically, not just the code. Use the data you gather to refine your next, more complex SaaS product.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation and the business strategy to stop guessing and start earning. Don't let this momentum fade into another abandoned project. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
